skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
stem:
Members of a fitness club were surveyed to determine whether they are satisfied with the locker rooms at the club. A random sample of 150 members was selected. All 150 responded, and 62% said they are satisfied. Based on this information, which of the following statements must be true?

I. Of all members of the fitness club, 62% would say they are satisfied with the locker rooms.
II. If another random sample of 150 members were surveyed, 62% would say they are satisfied.
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The 62% figure is a sample statistic, used to estimate the true population proportion. The population proportion need not be exactly 62%, and a second random sample need not yield exactly 62% satisfied. So neither statement must be true.

Choices A, B, and D incorrectly treat the sample statistic as a guaranteed exact match for the population or another sample.
